title: Mass payment failures — Visa cards declined with code 51 across checkout
description: Starting at 08:09 UTC, enterprise merchant reported a spike in Visa card declines. Decline code 51 (insufficient funds) appearing for cards that had confirmed available balances. Affected roughly 34% of Visa checkout attempts over a 20-minute window. Merchant support escalated after detecting a 6x increase in decline rate via their own dashboard.
root_cause: Downstream processor (Stripe Connect) was sending stale BIN data after a routing table refresh. A subset of Visa BINs was incorrectly mapped to a decommissioned acquiring bank endpoint, causing authorization requests to fail at the network level before reaching the issuer.
resolution: Rolled back processor routing table to last known-good snapshot. Issued advisory to affected merchant. Reprocessed 217 failed transactions via idempotency-safe retry job.

title: Webhook delivery delays — payment.succeeded events lagging 30-50 minutes
description: Enterprise merchant (multi-currency e-commerce) reported that their order fulfillment system was not receiving payment.succeeded webhooks in real-time. Events were delayed by 30-50 minutes, causing order processing queues to stall. Merchant had SLA-bound fulfillment obligations tied to webhook receipt.
root_cause: The webhook worker pool was exhausted due to a fan-out from a high-volume flash sale event. The queue depth grew unchecked because the autoscaler had a misconfigured cooldown period (10 minutes instead of 1 minute), preventing rapid scale-out. FIFO queue processing meant newer events waited behind a backlog of retries from transient 5xx responses on the merchant endpoint.
resolution: Manually scaled webhook worker fleet. Corrected autoscaler cooldown configuration. Introduced per-merchant queue isolation for Tier 1 merchants to prevent noisy-neighbor effects.

title: Subscription renewal charges failing silently — no error surfaced to merchant
description: Mid-market SaaS merchant noticed a drop in MRR reconciliation. On investigation, found that ~400 subscription renewals had been attempted but silently dropped. No retry was scheduled and no failure event was emitted. Merchant discovered the issue 18 hours later during manual revenue reconciliation.
root_cause: A deploy introduced a regression in the billing retry state machine. When a payment method tokenization call timed out (>3s), the exception was caught by an overly broad exception handler that logged the error but transitioned the renewal job to a terminal 'completed' state instead of 'failed'. Silent data loss with no alerting.
resolution: Hotfixed the state machine transition logic. Re-queued 400 affected renewal jobs. Added explicit alerting on renewal jobs that complete without a confirmed charge or explicit skip signal. Added narrow exception handling for tokenization timeouts.

title: 3DS2 challenge flow returning blank redirect on mobile Safari
description: Enterprise travel merchant reported that customers on iOS Safari were hitting a blank white screen during the 3DS2 challenge step, preventing checkout completion. Desktop browsers unaffected. Spike in support tickets beginning 11:30 UTC. Estimated ~12% of mobile checkout attempts affected.
root_cause: An upstream 3DS provider (Cardinal Commerce) updated their challenge iframe postMessage origin. Our CSP header whitelisted the old origin. Mobile Safari enforces stricter cross-origin iframe restrictions than Chrome/Firefox, so the postMessage from the challenge frame was silently dropped, leaving the challenge UI in a hung state with no timeout fallback.
resolution: Updated CSP headers to include new Cardinal origin. Added a 30-second timeout fallback that degrades to frictionless flow where issuer permits. Coordinated with Cardinal on advance notice for future origin changes.

title: Payout batch job stalled — merchants not receiving next-day ACH settlements
description: Multiple enterprise merchants contacted support that their scheduled next-day ACH payouts had not arrived. The payout batch job that runs at 05:00 UTC failed silently. No PagerDuty alert was triggered. Merchants discovered the issue independently when their bank reported no incoming wire.
root_cause: The payout batch job runs as a Kubernetes CronJob. A node drain event (routine maintenance) preempted the running job pod before it could complete. The CronJob's restartPolicy was set to OnFailure but the pod was not marked as failed — it was evicted, which counts as a deletion, not a failure. The job did not reschedule. No dead-letter alerting existed for evicted CronJob pods.
resolution: Manually triggered payout batch for affected merchants. Updated CronJob restartPolicy and added a pod disruption budget to prevent eviction during active payout windows. Added monitoring for CronJob completion status with PagerDuty integration.

title: Duplicate charges appearing on customer statements after retry logic triggered
description: SMB merchant received customer complaints about double charges. Platform retry logic had been triggered after a network timeout, but the original charge had succeeded at the processor level. The timeout occurred before the acknowledgment was received, leading to a second authorization and capture.
root_cause: Idempotency key scoping was incorrect in the retry wrapper. The key was generated using (merchant_id, amount, timestamp) rather than (merchant_id, idempotency_token_from_request). On timeout + retry, a new timestamp produced a new key, bypassing idempotency protection and issuing a second charge.
resolution: Fixed idempotency key generation to use the caller-supplied token. Added processor-level deduplication check (query charge status before retry). Issued refunds to 9 affected customers. Added integration test covering timeout + retry scenario.

title: HMAC signature validation failures causing merchant webhook handler to reject all events
description: Mid-market merchant reported that their webhook endpoint was rejecting all incoming events with 401 responses starting at 09:05 UTC. Event delivery was failing and retry backoff was growing. Order processing fully stalled downstream.
root_cause: A key rotation job cycled the webhook signing secret but did not propagate the new secret to the delivery workers within the same atomic operation. There was a ~6-minute window where workers were signing with the new secret while merchant documentation (and their validator) still referenced the old secret. Additionally, the key rotation was not announced to the merchant ahead of time.
resolution: Temporarily disabled signature validation for affected merchant to restore event flow. Pushed new secret to merchant via secure channel. Implemented dual-signing (sign with both old and new secret) during rotation window. Added 7-day advance notice policy for secret rotations.

title: Full payment processing outage for EUR-denominated transactions
description: All EUR-denominated payment attempts failing with a 500 error starting 03:40 UTC. Affected all merchants processing in EUR. Non-EUR transactions unaffected. High-severity alert triggered from our payment gateway health checks. Holiday peak traffic period.
root_cause: Currency conversion service had a stale FX rate cache. The cache TTL had been reduced from 15 minutes to 1 minute as part of a performance experiment, but the cache warming job was not updated to run at the new frequency. On cache miss, the service attempted a synchronous call to the FX rate provider, which was rate-limiting our IPs due to the new request frequency. With no fallback rate and no circuit breaker, requests errored out.
resolution: Reverted cache TTL to 15 minutes. Added circuit breaker with last-known-good rate fallback. Coordinated with FX provider to whitelist our IPs for higher request rates. Post-incident: deprecated the synchronous FX call path entirely in favor of async pre-warm.

title: Subscription plan migration incorrectly billing customers at old price tier
description: Enterprise SaaS merchant performed a bulk plan migration for ~2,000 customers from a legacy pricing tier to a new one. Customers were billed at the old price on their next renewal cycle, not the new price. Merchant discovered on review of the first post-migration billing run.
root_cause: The bulk plan migration job updated the subscription's plan_id in the primary database but the billing worker reads plan pricing from a read replica. The replica lag during the bulk write (which ran for ~4 minutes) meant some workers picked up renewals before the plan change was replicated. The billing worker did not revalidate pricing against the primary before charging.
resolution: Issued credits/adjustments for overbilled and underbilled customers. Updated billing worker to read pricing from primary for first renewal post-migration. Added a plan_migration_lock that prevents billing worker from processing subscriptions flagged as recently migrated until replication lag clears.

title: Apple Pay tokenization failing for all mid-market merchants on iOS 17.3
description: Following the iOS 17.3 release, mid-market merchants began reporting Apple Pay checkout failures. Customers on iOS 17.3 devices were seeing a generic 'payment not completed' error. iOS 17.2 and earlier unaffected. Issue affected all merchants using our Apple Pay integration.
root_cause: Apple changed the merchant validation endpoint domain format in iOS 17.3. Our Apple Pay session validation code used a hardcoded regex to validate the domain of the Apple merchant validation URL before making the session request. The new domain format failed the regex, causing us to reject the validation call before it was made.
resolution: Updated domain validation regex to match new Apple domain pattern. Deployed hotfix within 45 minutes of root cause identification. Coordinated with Apple partner team on future domain change notification process.

title: Fraud scoring service timeout causing all high-value transactions to be blocked
description: Starting 18:50 UTC, all transactions over $500 were being declined by the fraud risk engine. The risk engine was returning block decisions for 100% of high-value transactions rather than its normal ~2% block rate. Merchants reported high-value customer complaints immediately.
root_cause: The ML fraud scoring service experienced elevated latency due to a cold start on a new model version deployment. The risk engine has a 200ms timeout on the ML scoring call; on timeout, the fail-secure policy defaults to block. The deployment rolled out to 100% of traffic before the warm-up period completed, causing mass timeouts and mass blocks.
resolution: Rolled back fraud model deployment. Temporarily raised timeout threshold to 800ms to allow model to serve. Implemented canary deployment strategy for model rollouts with warm-up gate before traffic shift. Added separate timeout policy for transaction amounts above $1,000.

title: Webhook retry storm causing merchant endpoint DDoS-like load spike
description: An SMB merchant's webhook endpoint went down for 12 minutes due to a server restart on their end. When the endpoint recovered, our retry system delivered the backlogged events without rate limiting, sending ~4,000 events in under 60 seconds. The burst overwhelmed the merchant's server, causing a secondary outage.
root_cause: Webhook retry delivery used exponential backoff for delay calculation but did not implement a per-merchant delivery rate cap. When the endpoint recovered, all retries that had passed their backoff window were eligible for immediate delivery and were dispatched concurrently.
resolution: Implemented per-merchant webhook delivery rate cap (max 100 events/second). Added burst recovery mode that delivers backlog at a controlled rate when endpoint returns to healthy state. Coordinated with merchant to ensure their endpoint could handle the expected baseline rate.

title: Mastercard refunds failing with error code 70 — transaction not found at processor
description: Enterprise retail merchant reporting that Mastercard refunds initiated through the dashboard and API were failing at a high rate starting 13:35 UTC. Error code 70 from the processor indicates the original transaction cannot be located for the refund. Refunds for Visa and Amex unaffected.
root_cause: Mastercard requires refunds to reference the original acquirer reference number (ARN). A change in the processor's response schema renamed the field from acquirer_reference_number to arn in their API v3 response. Our integration had been updated to send to v3 but the ARN extraction logic still looked for the old field name, storing null. Refund attempts with a null ARN were rejected by the processor.
resolution: Fixed ARN field extraction to use new field name. Backfilled ARN values for the ~180 transactions that had null ARNs by querying the processor's transaction lookup API. Reprocessed affected refunds. Added schema contract tests for processor API responses.

title: Trial-to-paid conversion not charging customers — subscriptions stuck in trial state
description: Mid-market B2B SaaS merchant noticed that customers whose trials ended over the past 48 hours had not been converted to paid subscriptions. The trial end event was firing, but no charge was being initiated and the subscription status was not transitioning to active.
root_cause: A feature flag for a new dunning flow was enabled for a cohort that included this merchant. The new dunning flow required an explicit payment method confirmation step before trial conversion, but the UI prompt for that step had not been released. Customers were silently stuck waiting for a confirmation that was never presented.
resolution: Disabled the new dunning feature flag for affected merchant. Manually triggered trial conversion for 63 subscriptions. Implemented feature flag dependency validation to prevent enabling backend flows that depend on unreleased frontend steps.

title: Network tokenization refresh failing — stored card tokens expiring and triggering declines
description: Enterprise subscription merchant saw a sharp increase in decline rates on recurring charges starting 20:10 UTC. Cards that had previously been successfully charged were now declining with code 54 (expired card) despite the underlying card not being expired. Issue affected all recurring charges using network tokens.
root_cause: Network token lifecycle management job failed to refresh tokens nearing expiry. The refresh job queries the token requestor service (Visa Token Service / Mastercard MDES) for updated token cryptograms. A certificate used to authenticate to the token requestor service expired at 20:00 UTC. The refresh job swallowed the authentication error and marked tokens as refreshed without actually updating the cryptogram.
resolution: Renewed the client certificate. Re-ran token refresh job for all tokens flagged as expiring within 30 days. Added certificate expiry monitoring with 30-day advance alerting. Fixed error handling in refresh job to surface authentication failures rather than masking them.

title: API rate limit exhaustion causing payment requests to queue indefinitely
description: Enterprise marketplace merchant reported that payment creation requests were returning 429 errors starting 17:25 UTC. The merchant runs a high-frequency marketplace model with bursts of concurrent payments. Requests were being dropped rather than queued, causing missed sales during a promotional event.
root_cause: The merchant's API key was shared across three separate microservices in their integration. Each service had its own retry loop with no shared rate limit awareness. During the promotional burst, all three services simultaneously hit the rate cap and entered overlapping retry cycles, amplifying the request rate rather than backing off. Our platform enforced a hard cap with no burst allowance.
resolution: Issued a dedicated API key per service to distribute rate limit allocation. Enabled burst capacity headroom for enterprise-tier keys during pre-approved event windows. Provided the merchant with client-side rate limiting guidance and a token bucket implementation reference. Added rate limit monitoring alerts to detect 429 spikes before they cascade.

title: OAuth access token expiry causing payment API calls to silently fail
description: Mid-market merchant's backend integration stopped creating payments at 11:00 UTC. API calls were returning 401 Unauthorized. Merchant had no alerting on 401 response codes, so the issue was discovered when order management showed a payment creation gap of 65 minutes.
root_cause: The merchant was caching their OAuth access token without handling token refresh. The token's 1-hour TTL expired, and the integration was not configured to catch 401 responses and trigger a re-authentication flow. The 401 errors were logged client-side but no alert was attached to the log pattern.
resolution: Guided merchant through implementing a token refresh loop with proactive re-authentication at 80% of TTL. Added a platform-side 'token_expiry_warning' webhook event that fires 5 minutes before token expiry for integrations using long-lived tokens. Updated API documentation with OAuth best practices and retry-on-401 pseudocode.

title: Geo-routing misconfiguration sending EU merchant traffic to US processing endpoint
description: EU-based enterprise merchant reported elevated latency (>8s per payment) and intermittent failures starting 04:10 UTC. Payments were completing but taking 8-12 seconds instead of the expected <1s. EU card issuers were returning timeouts before authorization completed.
root_cause: A DNS weight update intended to shift 10% of EU traffic to a new Frankfurt edge node instead set the US-East endpoint weight to 100%, routing all EU traffic transatlantically. The round-trip latency to the US endpoint exceeded the issuer authorization timeout threshold for several EU issuing banks. The misconfiguration was introduced by a CDN configuration change that didn't validate regional weight totals.
resolution: Reverted CDN routing weights. Validated all regional routing tables. Implemented a configuration validation step that checks regional weight totals sum to 100% before applying. Added latency-based health checks that alert when p99 payment authorization latency exceeds 2s in any region.

title: SEPA bank transfer failures — incorrect IBAN checksum validation rejecting valid accounts
description: Enterprise merchant processing SEPA payouts to EU beneficiary accounts reported a high failure rate starting 09:50 UTC. Approximately 38% of SEPA Credit Transfer (SCT) submissions were being rejected at the validation layer before reaching the banking partner. Rejection reason: 'invalid IBAN format'.
root_cause: A library upgrade (iban-validator v2.1.0 → v3.0.0) changed the checksum validation logic to apply a stricter MOD97 check that incorrectly rejected IBANs from three EU countries (Finland, Malta, Slovenia) due to a bug in the country-specific length table. The library version was pinned in a transitive dependency and was bumped silently during a routine dependency update.
resolution: Pinned iban-validator to v2.1.0 pending upstream bug fix. Re-submitted 312 rejected SEPA transfers. Added integration tests that validate IBANs from all 36 SEPA-participating countries against the official IBAN registry. Added a dependency change notification check to the CI pipeline to flag transitive version bumps on financial validation libraries.

title: Chargeback dispute webhook events not firing — merchant missing dispute window
description: Enterprise merchant's dispute management system stopped receiving chargeback.created webhook events 14 days ago. The merchant discovered this when manually reviewing their bank portal and finding 23 unresponded disputes, several of which had already passed the response window. Revenue exposure from undefended chargebacks estimated at ~$34,000.
root_cause: A webhook event schema migration added a new required field (dispute_evidence_due_at) to the chargeback.created payload. The delivery worker validated outgoing payloads against the new schema before dispatch. Existing chargeback events that were queued before the migration were missing this field, causing schema validation to fail and the event to be dropped without a dead-letter queue or alerting on the drop.
resolution: Backfilled dispute_evidence_due_at for all queued events using chargeback metadata. Delivered the 23 missed events (noting which had expired windows). Added a dead-letter queue for schema validation failures with immediate alerting. Added backward-compatibility requirement to webhook schema migrations: new required fields must have default values for a minimum 30-day transition period.

title: Proration calculation incorrect after mid-cycle plan upgrade — customers overcharged
description: SMB SaaS merchant received customer complaints about unexpected charge amounts after mid-cycle plan upgrades. Customers upgrading from a monthly $29 plan to a monthly $79 plan mid-cycle were being charged the full $79 rather than the prorated difference for the remaining cycle days.
root_cause: The proration calculation function used integer division to compute the per-day rate, which truncated fractional cents and accumulated rounding error. For a 31-day month, the per-day rate of $29/31 was truncated to $0.93 instead of $0.935..., causing the proration credit to be understated. Additionally, the remaining-days counter included the current day (which had already been paid), resulting in a double-charge for the upgrade day.
resolution: Switched proration calculation to use Python's Decimal type with ROUND_HALF_EVEN for monetary amounts. Fixed the off-by-one error in the remaining-days counter. Issued refunds to 47 affected customers (average overcharge: $1.82). Added property-based tests for proration across all plan combinations and cycle lengths.

title: 3D Secure fallback loop causing card-not-present transactions to fail repeatedly
description: Enterprise merchant reported that a subset of customers were stuck in an infinite 3DS retry loop at checkout. Customers were being redirected to the 3DS authentication page, completing authentication successfully, but then being redirected back to the same page again rather than completing the transaction.
root_cause: The 3DS session token was stored server-side with a 5-minute TTL. For customers on slow mobile connections, the authentication completion callback took longer than 5 minutes to reach our server. By the time the callback arrived, the session token had expired, causing the payment to be re-initiated from scratch with a new 3DS challenge rather than completing the original authorization. No maximum retry cap existed.
resolution: Extended 3DS session token TTL from 5 to 15 minutes. Added a maximum retry cap of 3 per payment intent, after which the session is abandoned with an explicit error rather than restarted. Added p99 latency tracking on 3DS callback receipt to detect timeout patterns by geography and mobile carrier.

title: Velocity rules incorrectly flagging legitimate bulk B2B payment batches as fraud
description: Mid-market B2B payments platform reported that their end-of-month bulk payment batches were being flagged as suspicious and held for manual review. Approximately 340 legitimate B2B payments from known corporate customers were blocked, delaying supplier payments.
root_cause: The velocity rule engine evaluated transaction counts per card number per hour. For corporate cards used in B2B bulk payment scenarios, legitimate end-of-month batches naturally exceeded the 50-transactions-per-card-per-hour threshold set for consumer fraud detection. The merchant had not been onboarded with a 'B2B bulk payment' risk profile that would apply a higher threshold for verified corporate card BINs.
resolution: Applied B2B bulk payment risk profile to the merchant, raising the velocity threshold for corporate BINs to 500 transactions per hour. Released the 340 held payments after manual review. Added merchant segment tagging to the risk onboarding flow to prevent consumer velocity rules from applying to B2B merchants by default.

title: Payment link expiry not enforced — customers charged on links expired over 30 days ago
description: Enterprise merchant noticed payments being collected through URLs generated over 30 days ago after their configured expiry period. Link expiry was set to 7 days in the merchant dashboard. Transactions were completing successfully, bypassing the expiry check entirely.
root_cause: The payment link expiry check was implemented in the frontend rendering layer — the link URL would return a 'link expired' page in the browser — but the underlying payment creation API endpoint had no server-side expiry validation. Customers who had saved or bookmarked the raw API URL could bypass the UI expiry message and submit payments directly.
resolution: Added server-side expiry validation to the payment link creation endpoint. Voided all payments completed through expired links (after merchant review). Implemented signed expiry timestamps in the link URL that are verified on the server before payment intent creation. Added audit logging for all payment link submissions including age at time of use.

title: Event ordering violation — refund.succeeded delivered before payment.succeeded for same transaction
description: Enterprise merchant's order management system entered an inconsistent state when webhook events arrived out of order. The refund.succeeded event for a transaction was delivered before the payment.succeeded event, causing their state machine to receive a refund event for a payment it had never seen, resulting in orphaned refund records and corrupted order state.
root_cause: The webhook delivery system processed events from a shared queue ordered by event creation timestamp. However, payment.succeeded and refund.succeeded were published to the queue by different services with independent clock sources. The refund event (generated by the refund service) had a slightly earlier timestamp than the payment event (generated by the payment service) due to clock skew between the two service hosts (~340ms). FIFO ordering on timestamp caused the refund event to be dispatched first.
resolution: Switched event ordering for related events to use a logical sequence number (monotonically increasing, scoped per payment_intent_id) rather than wall-clock timestamp. Added a dependency graph for event types — refund events now wait for a confirmed payment.succeeded delivery before being dispatched. Deployed NTP synchronization audit across all event-producing services.
